message:
mcp-server v0.3 + CI tail (one tighter loop, three deliverables)

## v0.3d — mutation tools + capability gating + refresh intent

- New tier system: --capabilities read|internal|publish|admin (CSV).
  read = default (rulake_query + list_backends only).
  publish adds: rulake_publish_bundle, rulake_refresh_from_bundle_dir.
  admin   adds: rulake_save_cache_to_dir, rulake_warm_from_dir, rulake_invalidate_cache.
  Cap-check fires at tools/call time: ungranted → RULAKE_CAPABILITY_REFUSED.
- intent: "refresh" wired through planner → refresh_from_bundle_dir,
  capability-gated at the tool layer (Capability::Publish).
- 3 new capability-gating smoke tests + the existing 18 = 21 pass.
- Wire-verified end-to-end: read mode trying to call rulake_publish_bundle
  returns RULAKE_CAPABILITY_REFUSED with the exact error message.

## v0.3e — JSONL audit file with §7 schema

- New `audit` module: `AuditSink::stderr()` (default) | `open_file(path)`.
- Schema mirrors ADR-004 §7 outer block: ts (ISO-8601 UTC, hand-rolled
  to avoid pulling chrono), transport, principal, session, request_id,
  tool, intent, outcome, result_size, trust_level, duration_ms,
  witness_in/out, code, plus policy_decision (capability_required +
  granted) and decision (full planner trace as nested JSON).
- --audit-file PATH switches the sink to append-only JSONL with
  per-line flush. Each line has policy_decision + decision blocks
  populated — the §7 acceptance gate ("every audit line explains itself").
- 3 audit unit tests (ts format, epoch math, file roundtrip).
- Live e2e: a `tools/call rulake_query intent=explain` against
  rulake-mcp stdio --audit-file /tmp/audit.jsonl writes a complete
  §7 record (verified by parsing).

## CI tail — three more workflows

- release-rust.yml: cargo publish on tag rust-v* (gated on
  CRATES_IO_TOKEN secret; dry-run by default). Submodules: recursive
  so the vendored rabitq path-dep packages cleanly.
- Dockerfile.mcp: distroless multi-stage. Builder = rust:bookworm
  with BuildKit cache mounts; runtime = gcr.io/distroless/cc-debian12:nonroot.
  ~25 MB image, no shell, EXPOSE 7440, ENTRYPOINT rulake-mcp / CMD stdio.
- release-docker.yml: builds + pushes to ghcr.io/ruvnet/rulake-mcp on
  tag mcp-v*. Uses GITHUB_TOKEN (no extra secret).
- release-mcp-bin.yml: per-platform cargo build (5 triples) → tar.gz/zip
  → softprops/action-gh-release@v2 attached to the GitHub Release.

All 6 workflow YAMLs parse clean (yaml.safe_load).

## Verification

- cargo build --release   → silent (1 unused-variable warning, harmless)
- cargo test --release     → 21/21 pass
- ./rulake-mcp --capabilities read,publish,admin lists all 7 tools ✓
- read mode + tools/call rulake_publish_bundle → RULAKE_CAPABILITY_REFUSED ✓
- --audit-file /tmp/x.jsonl writes per-line records with policy_decision +
  decision blocks; ts is ISO-8601, fields match §7 ✓

Bumped mcp-server to v0.3.0.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+# Release the rulake-mcp Docker image to GHCR.
+#
+# Triggered manually or on tag `mcp-v*`. Builds the distroless image
+# defined by Dockerfile.mcp and pushes:
+#   ghcr.io/ruvnet/rulake-mcp:<tag>     (e.g. mcp-v0.3.0)
+#   ghcr.io/ruvnet/rulake-mcp:latest    (on tag push, not workflow_dispatch)
+#
+# Uses the GITHUB_TOKEN's packages:write scope — no extra secrets needed.
+
+name: release-docker
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "mcp-v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      tag:
+        description: "Image tag (default: dev)"
+        type: string
+        default: dev
+
+env:
+  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
+  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository_owner }}/rulake-mcp
+
+jobs:
+  build-and-push:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
+      packages: write
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
+        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
+      - name: Log in to GHCR
+        uses: docker/login-action@v3
+        with:
+          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
+          username: ${{ github.actor }}
+          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+      - name: Compute tags
+        id: meta
+        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
+        with:
+          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
+          tags: |
+            type=raw,value=${{ github.event.inputs.tag || 'latest' }},enable=${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/mcp-v') }}
+            type=match,pattern=mcp-v(.*),group=1
+            type=sha,prefix=sha-
+      - name: Build + push
+        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
+        with:
+          context: .
+          file: Dockerfile.mcp
+          push: true
+          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
+          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
+          cache-from: type=gha
+          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

@@ -0,0 +1,94 @@
+# Release rulake-mcp prebuilt binaries to GitHub Releases.
+#
+# Triggered manually or on tag `mcp-v*`. Builds rulake-mcp for the
+# five core platforms (matches the SDKs' coverage) and uploads each
+# as a tarball/zip to the GitHub Release for the tag.
+
+name: release-mcp-bin
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "mcp-v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  PACKAGE_DIR: mcp-server
+  BIN: rulake-mcp
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    name: build (${{ matrix.target.triple }})
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.target.os }}
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        target:
+          - { os: ubuntu-latest,   triple: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  archive: tar.gz }
+          - { os: ubuntu-latest,   triple: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, archive: tar.gz, cross: true }
+          - { os: macos-13,        triple: x86_64-apple-darwin,        archive: tar.gz }
+          - { os: macos-14,        triple: aarch64-apple-darwin,       archive: tar.gz }
+          - { os: windows-latest,  triple: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,     archive: zip }
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+        with:
+          targets: ${{ matrix.target.triple }}
+      - name: Cross-compile shim (linux-aarch64)
+        if: matrix.target.cross == true
+        run: |
+          sudo apt-get update
+          sudo apt-get install -y gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu
+          echo 'CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_GNU_LINKER=aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc' >> $GITHUB_ENV
+      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          workspaces: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+      - name: Build
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target.triple }} --bin ${{ env.BIN }}
+      - name: Stage artifact (unix)
+        if: matrix.target.archive == 'tar.gz'
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          mkdir -p staging
+          cp target/${{ matrix.target.triple }}/release/${{ env.BIN }} staging/
+          cp ../README.md ../LICENSE-* staging/ 2>/dev/null || true
+          tar -czf ${{ env.BIN }}-${{ matrix.target.triple }}.tar.gz -C staging .
+      - name: Stage artifact (windows)
+        if: matrix.target.archive == 'zip'
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          mkdir -p staging
+          cp target/${{ matrix.target.triple }}/release/${{ env.BIN }}.exe staging/
+          cp ../README.md staging/ 2>/dev/null || true
+          7z a ${{ env.BIN }}-${{ matrix.target.triple }}.zip ./staging/*
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: bin-${{ matrix.target.triple }}
+          path: |
+            ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}/${{ env.BIN }}-${{ matrix.target.triple }}.tar.gz
+            ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}/${{ env.BIN }}-${{ matrix.target.triple }}.zip
+
+  release:
+    name: attach to GitHub Release
+    needs: build
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/mcp-v')
+    permissions:
+      contents: write
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          path: dist
+          merge-multiple: true
+      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
+        with:
+          files: |
+            dist/*.tar.gz
+            dist/*.zip
+          generate_release_notes: true

@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+# Release the Rust crate `ruvector-rulake` to crates.io.
+#
+# Triggered manually or on tag `rust-v*`. Per ADR-001, the parent
+# crate depends on `vendor/ruvector/crates/ruvector-rabitq` via a
+# git-submodule path-dep. crates.io publish requires that the path
+# dep be embedded in the published .crate (cargo handles this with
+# `cargo package --no-verify` since the submodule is on disk after
+# `actions/checkout` recursive).
+#
+# Gated on CRATES_IO_TOKEN secret. Dry-run by default — no-op
+# unless explicitly opted in.
+
+name: release-rust
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "rust-v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      publish:
+        description: "Publish to crates.io (uncheck for dry-run)"
+        type: boolean
+        default: false
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+
+jobs:
+  publish:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive   # vendor/ruvector must be present
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+      - name: Build + test
+        run: |
+          cargo build --release
+          cargo test --release
+      - name: Package (sanity check; doesn't publish)
+        run: cargo package --allow-dirty --no-verify
+      - name: Publish to crates.io
+        if: |
+          startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/rust-v') ||
+          (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.publish == 'true')
+        env:
+          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CRATES_IO_TOKEN }}
+        run: cargo publish --allow-dirty --no-verify

@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+# rulake-mcp — distroless image for the MCP server (ADR-004 §8).
+#
+# Multi-stage:
+# 1. `builder` — full Rust toolchain on Debian, builds rulake-mcp
+#    in --release. Uses BuildKit cache mounts to keep cargo-deps
+#    builds fast on re-runs.
+# 2. `runtime` — distroless/cc — just glibc + ca-certificates +
+#    the binary. ~25 MB image, no shell, no package manager.
+#
+# Build:  docker build -f Dockerfile.mcp -t rulake-mcp .
+# Run:    docker run --rm -p 7440:7440 \
+#             -v /etc/rulake/mcp.toml:/etc/rulake/mcp.toml:ro \
+#             rulake-mcp http --bind 0.0.0.0:7440 \
+#                              --config /etc/rulake/mcp.toml \
+#                              --auth bearer --bearer-token-file /etc/rulake/token \
+#                              --allow-bearer-on-public
+
+# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7
+
+FROM rust:1-bookworm AS builder
+WORKDIR /work
+
+# Submodule sources (per ADR-001 the rabitq kernel comes from vendor/).
+# The .dockerignore excludes target/ so the build is hermetic.
+COPY . .
+
+# BuildKit cache mounts: the registry index, downloaded crates, and
+# the per-target build artifacts all survive between rebuilds.
+RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/local/cargo/registry \
+    --mount=type=cache,target=/work/mcp-server/target \
+    cargo build --release --manifest-path mcp-server/Cargo.toml \
+    && cp mcp-server/target/release/rulake-mcp /usr/local/bin/rulake-mcp
+
+# ── runtime stage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+FROM gcr.io/distroless/cc-debian12:nonroot
+COPY --from=builder /usr/local/bin/rulake-mcp /usr/local/bin/rulake-mcp
+
+EXPOSE 7440
+USER nonroot:nonroot
+
+# Default to stdio so the image is a drop-in MCP server for parent
+# processes; HTTP requires explicit args.
+ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/rulake-mcp"]
+CMD ["stdio"]

@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@
 
 [package]
 name = "ruvector-rulake-mcp"
-version = "0.2.0"
+version = "0.3.0"
 edition = "2021"
 license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
 authors = ["Ruvector Team"]

@@ -4,32 +4,36 @@ Implements [ADR-004](../docs/adrs/sdk/ADR-004-rulake-mcp-server.md) — the
 control-plane / decision-layer over [`ruvector-rulake`](..) for
 agent-callable governed memory.
 
-## Status: v0.2 (deployable to remote agents)
-
-v0.1 landed the architecture; v0.2 makes it deployable to remote
-agents (Streamable HTTP transport + bearer auth with the
-embarrassing-flag dev-only guards from ADR-004 §5).
-
-| Capability | v0.1 | v0.2 | v0.3 |
-|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
-| stdio transport                              | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| `rulake_query` (intent: `search`)            | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| `rulake_list_backends`                       | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| Bounded worker pool (`flume` + `rayon`)      | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| Decision trace (`reason_code`, `decision.*`) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| TOML config (`mcp.toml`) — backends, consistency, workers | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| Witness-fail-closed for bundle reads         | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
-| Streamable HTTP transport                    |    | ✅ | ✅ |
-| Bearer-token auth (dev-only, embarrassing-flag for public bind) | | ✅ | ✅ |
-| DNS-rebinding guard (rmcp `allowed_hosts`)   |    | ✅ | ✅ |
-| OAuth 2.1 + mTLS                             |    |    | ✅ |
-| Replay protection (`MCP-Request-Id` + session binding) | | | ✅ |
-| Layered rate limiting (`governor`)           |    |    | ✅ |
-| Intents `verify` / `explain`                 |    |    | ✅ |
-| Resources (`rulake://stats`, `rulake://bundle/...`) | | | ✅ |
-| Mutation tools (`publish`, `admin` capabilities) |  |    | ✅ |
-| `intent: "refresh"`                          |    |    | ✅ |
-| JSONL audit file (full §7 schema)            |    |    | ✅ |
+## Status: v0.3 (production-shaped surface)
+
+v0.1 landed the architecture, v0.2 added remote-agent deployability
+(Streamable HTTP + bearer), v0.3 ships the rest of the ADR-004 §4 +
+§5 + §7 surface: mutation tools behind capability tiers, all four
+intents (search/verify/explain/refresh), the §7 JSONL audit file,
+and `rulake://stats` resources.
+
+| Capability | v0.1 | v0.2 | v0.3 | v0.4 |
+|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
+| stdio transport                              | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| `rulake_query` (intent: `search`)            | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| `rulake_list_backends`                       | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Bounded worker pool (`flume` + `rayon`)      | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Decision trace (`reason_code`, `decision.*`) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| TOML config (`mcp.toml`)                     | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Witness-fail-closed for bundle reads         | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Streamable HTTP transport                    |    | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Bearer-token auth (dev-only, embarrassing-flag for public bind) | | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| DNS-rebinding guard (rmcp `allowed_hosts`)   |    | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Intents `verify` / `explain`                 |    |    | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Resources (`rulake://stats`, `rulake://stats/by-backend`) | | | ✅ | ✅ |
+| Mutation tools (`publish`, `admin` capability tiers) |  |    | ✅ | ✅ |
+| `intent: "refresh"`                          |    |    | ✅ | ✅ |
+| JSONL audit file (`--audit-file`, full §7 schema) |  |    | ✅ | ✅ |
+| OAuth 2.1 + mTLS                             |    |    |    | ✅ |
+| Replay protection (`MCP-Request-Id` + session binding) | | | | ✅ |
+| Layered rate limiting (`governor`)           |    |    |    | ✅ |
+| Resource: `rulake://bundle/{backend}/{collection}` | | |   | ✅ |
+| `tools/list` filtered by capability          |    |    |    | ✅ |
 
 ## Build
